rationale: Both correctly diagnose the missing-extended-ratio-token crash and the base-vs-Instruct config mismatch, matching the thread; both cite the confirmed DiT workaround and defer the hardening work to a follow-up issue per Gaohan123. X is more complete, explicitly covering both thread proposals (docs fix on misleading deploy-YAML headers, plus a guard) with specific line-level citations, whereas Y's fix section, while cleaner, only addresses the guard and skips the docs-clarity ask. X's fix logic is slightly muddled (proposing both a tolerant empty-list fallback and a hard fail, which are redundant/contradictory) while Y's single fail-fast ValueError more crisply matches the maintainer's stated intent, but this is offset by X's broader coverage and more granular grounding claims.
